summary.intent: Fix multiple bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension: server port conflicts on reload, missing database schema on startup, checkpoints not showing in the treeview, and per-repo DB isolation for a multi-repo architecture.
summary.outcome: Fixed all major issues: orphaned server killing, schema push comment-filter bug, per-repo DB registry replacing single primary DB, gitlog maxBuffer overflow causing missing branch history, session sorting by workspace, and minor issues like worktree --detach, LICENSE.txt, and brew install command.

repo: Each repo has its own SQLite DB — there is no 'primary' DB hierarchy. The server was incorrectly treating the startup --repo-dir DB as primary.
schema.sql is generated by Prisma and every SQL chunk starts with a '-- CreateTable' comment, causing the old !s.startsWith('--') filter to silently discard all 56 statements.
Shadow branches are written by the Entire CLI background process, not git hooks. If the Entire watcher dies, no new shadow branches or checkpoint commits are written.
Checkpoint commits use 'Entire-Checkpoint:' trailers on feature branches, not on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch. findCommitForCheckpoint correctly searches feature branches.
The gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus: true (adds --name-status), which causes stdout maxBuffer exceeded on large repos with many commits, silently swallowing the error in saveGitOidMapping.
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr can cause SQLite 'disk I/O error' on newly created DB files; solution is to delete and recreate the file.
Git worktrees cannot check out the same branch twice; multiple repos sharing entire/checkpoints/v1 requires --detach flag when creating the checkpoint worktree.
The Entire CLI hook for cosmos-graphql is configured in project-level .claude/settings.json, not global ~/.claude/settings.json.

code.finding: pushSchema was only called for the primary DB; secondary repo DBs registered from config never had schema applied at startup.
The old pushSchema filtered chunks with !s.startsWith('--'), which silently discarded all SQL statements because every Prisma-generated chunk starts with '-- CreateTable'.
findCommitForCheckpoint used gitlog with default nameStatus: true, causing maxBuffer exceeded on large repos. Fix: nameStatus: false and maxBuffer: 100MB.
saveGitOidMapping catch block swallows ALL errors silently, hiding the maxBuffer overflow that caused 0 git OID mappings.
saveGitOidMapping is skipped when mappedCheckpointIds already contains the ID, so corrupt/empty prior runs block re-indexing.
spawnServer only checked the in-memory GossamerPanel.server static; orphaned processes from crashed sessions held the port invisibly.
GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints used the single 'db' primary client; queries for sessions in other repos returned empty arrays.

workflow: When a schema push silently does nothing, test the raw SQL pipeline in isolation (split → filter → execute) before assuming the DB or connection is broken.
Compiled dist/ can diverge from source during iterative fixes; always test against the compiled output when debugging runtime behavior.
Use lsof to find and SIGTERM orphaned processes holding a port rather than adopting stale servers.
The gitlog npm package is unsuitable for large repos at high commit counts without nameStatus: false and a large maxBuffer override.
SQLite files with 0 tables but non-zero size (4096 bytes = just the header) indicate the file was opened/created but schema never applied.

friction: Multiple rounds of schema push debugging because each fix revealed the next silent failure (comment filter → macOS xattr → per-repo not applied at startup).
The single 'db' primary client was deeply embedded throughout server.ts, requiring ~20 individual endpoint edits to migrate to the dbRegistry pattern.
gitlog's maxBuffer failure was caught and silently discarded, making it appear as if findCommitForCheckpoint was working but returning null legitimately.
The Entire CLI background watcher appears to have been broken for 3+ weeks with no new shadow branches or checkpoint commits being written, making it impossible to test live session update flows.
GET /api/repos/current only matched the server's startup repoDir, causing every non-primary workspace to appear unregistered and trigger redundant re-registration.
resolveDb probe for GET /api/sessions/:id only checked shadowSession, missing sessions that only existed in checkpointSessionMetadata or logEvent tables.
open_items: Entire CLI background watcher appears dead — no new shadow branches or checkpoint commits in 3+ weeks for cosmos-graphql. Root cause not investigated.
The restart path in GossamerPanel still doesn't wait for the old server to fully die before spawning a new one (noted but deferred).
Gossamer could surface sessions that exist only as .entire/metadata/ files on disk (no shadow branch yet) as a fallback for incomplete Entire CLI data.
9 of 17 checkpoint git OID mappings were created for cosmos-graphql after the gitlog fix; the remaining 8 were not investigated (possibly on branches beyond 10k commit depth).
The webview API calls (fetchSession, fetchLogEvents, fetchCheckpoints) still don't pass localPath for session-specific resolution — they rely on resolveDb probing all DBs, which works but is slower.
Live session updates for resumed sessions depend entirely on the Entire CLI writing new shadow branch commits; Gossamer has no mechanism to detect or surface this failure to the user.
